title: Operators & Expressions
description: Learn how to use operators to perform calculations and comparisons.

blocks.content:
Arithmetic operators perform mathematical calculations:

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| + | Addition | `10 + 5` | 15 |
| - | Subtraction | `10 - 5` | 5 |
| * | Multiplication | `10 * 5` | 50 |
| / | Division | `10 / 5` | 2 |
| % | Modulo (remainder) | `10 % 3` | 1 |
| ** | Exponentiation | `2 ** 3` | 8 |

You can combine these in expressions: `(5 + 3) * 2 = 16`

blocks.title: Arithmetic Practice

blocks.content:
Comparison operators compare two values and return true or false:

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|----------|
| == | Equal (loose) | `5 == "5"` returns true |
| === | Equal (strict) | `5 === "5"` returns false |
| != | Not equal | `5 != 3` returns true |
| !== | Not equal (strict) | `5 !== "5"` returns true |
| > | Greater than | `10 > 5` returns true |
| < | Less than | `3 < 5` returns true |
| >= | Greater or equal | `5 >= 5` returns true |
| <= | Less or equal | `3 <= 5` returns true |

**Pro Tip**: Always use `===` and `!==` instead of `==` and `!=`!

blocks.title: Comparison Practice

blocks.content:
Logical operators combine boolean values:

| Operator | Name | Meaning | Example |
|----------|------|---------|----------|
| && | AND | Both must be true | `(5 > 3) && (3 > 1)` = true |
| \|\| | OR | At least one must be true | `(5 > 3) \|\| (1 > 3)` = true |
| ! | NOT | Reverses true/false | `!(5 > 3)` = false |

These are essential for creating complex conditions in your code.

blocks.title: Logical Operators
